author: @nothingtoseehear5012
text: Sadly Mi8 should be called 'Mission Implied'...all of the grandeur and action and threat is implied for the first entire half of the movie (over 1 hour). For example, in the first 10 minutes there is a fight scene where we only hear the fight as we watch Grace's face react to it. Then when the fight noises stop, Tom Cruise walks into frame and says 'Oh boy, that sure was a tough fight. Did you see the kicks and flips? I hit one guy with an axe, did you see that? The the other guy tore my shirt. Well, I'm Ethan Hunt, and I guess we should get out of here.' Then the next hour of the movie other agents and cast members from past movies talk about what a bad ass Ethan Hunt is by saying 'He's a badass. Here is a clip from Mi1.' Someone else walks into the scene and says, 'Oh, Ethan Hunt? He's a badass. Here is a clip from Mi4'. Then someone else walks into the scene and says 'Ethan Hunt? I heard he's a badass. Here's a clip from just 10 days ago from the last movie Mi7.'  (I really do wish I was joking) Almost all of the intrigue and plot and stakes and even the action to a large degree is 'implied'. Someone tells you a story on screen and you as the audience members are supposed to imagine it? I guess? Mi8 gives off 'we half tried' vibes is all I'm saying.

author: @christexas4683
text:
I Loved the movie.  Also, as a counterpoint to a complaint both the Drinker and Nerdrotic made, it is the culmination of three-decades worth of stories. OF COURSE they are gonna have call-backs to those previous movies. They were showing Ethan Hunt's character arc, from the first movie where he is thrust into responsibility for a team that has been butchered, all the way to this grand finale. The story might be a bit silly, but it's a Mission Impossible movie -- of course it's a bit convoluted and silly. The story exists to get us to the next stunt or moving moment. And as for moving moments, there were plenty. I saw it in a crowded theatre and there were a few scenes where I could here quiet sniffling around me.

And let's not forget: "We live and die in the shadows, for those we hold close, and those we never meet."  This oft-repeated statement was not just something the members of the IMF tell themselves in the movie, it is a message from Tom Cruise (and the other actors) to the audience watching in a dark theatre. Their characters live and die in those shadows, and they are doing their best for that audience, most of whom they will never meet. 

So yes, mark me down as a huge fan. And thank you Tom Cruise and everyone involved with this incredible franchise over the last three decades, It's been a hell of a ride.
